title: GPU Frustum & Occlusion Culling on Meshlets
description: Extends GPU culling to the meshlet granularity used in Nanite: the amplification shader in a mesh shader pipeline performs per-meshlet frustum culling (cone culling for back-facing clusters, sphere test for frustum) and Hi-Z occlusion test before emitting meshlets for actual rasterization. Per-meshlet culling is orders of magnitude finer than per-object culling and eliminates over 95% of rasterization work in typical scenes when combined with backface cone culling. Students will implement meshlet cone culling in an amplification shader, add Hi-Z occlusion test, and measure the meshlet-to-primitive ratio improvement versus per-object culling on a test scene.
